items.name: sodium chloride
items.student_answer: NaCl is sodium chloride, also known as table salt. It is an ionic compound made from sodium ions and chloride ions. It is ionic, not covalent.
misconceptions: NaCl is not a covalent compound.
NaCl is better described as a formula unit, not a molecule.
The subscript 1 is understood and not written.

items.name: water
items.student_answer: H2O is water. It is a covalent compound made from hydrogen and oxygen.
misconceptions: The 2 means there are two hydrogen atoms for every one oxygen atom.

items.name: carbon dioxide
items.student_answer: CO2 is carbon dioxide. It is a covalent compound made from carbon and oxygen.
misconceptions: The 2 only applies to oxygen, not carbon.
CO2 is different from CO, which is carbon monoxide.

items.name: oxygen gas
items.student_answer: O2 is oxygen gas. It is a diatomic element, which means two oxygen atoms are bonded together.
misconceptions: O2 is not the same as a single oxygen atom.
O2 is an element form, not a compound.

items.name: hydrogen gas
items.student_answer: H2 is hydrogen gas. It is a diatomic element, which means two hydrogen atoms are bonded together.

items.name: nitrogen gas
items.student_answer: N2 is nitrogen gas. It is a diatomic element, which means two nitrogen atoms are bonded together.

items.name: chlorine gas
items.student_answer: Cl2 is chlorine gas. It is a diatomic element, which means two chlorine atoms are bonded together.

items.name: ammonia
items.student_answer: NH3 is ammonia. It is a covalent compound made from nitrogen and hydrogen.

items.name: methane
items.student_answer: CH4 is methane. It is a covalent compound made from carbon and hydrogen.

items.name: glucose
items.student_answer: C6H12O6 is glucose. It is a sugar made from carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen.

items.name: calcium chloride
items.student_answer: CaCl2 is calcium chloride. It is an ionic compound made from calcium ions and chloride ions.

items.name: magnesium oxide
items.student_answer: MgO is magnesium oxide. It is an ionic compound made from magnesium ions and oxide ions.

items.name: hydrochloric acid
items.student_answer: HCl is commonly taught as hydrochloric acid when it is dissolved in water. It contains hydrogen and chlorine.

items.name: sodium hydroxide
items.student_answer: NaOH is sodium hydroxide. It is an ionic compound made from sodium ions and hydroxide ions.

items.name: hydrogen peroxide
items.student_answer: H2O2 is hydrogen peroxide. It is a covalent compound made from hydrogen and oxygen.

items.name: calcium carbonate
items.student_answer: CaCO3 is calcium carbonate. It is an ionic compound that contains calcium ions and carbonate ions.

items.name: sulfur dioxide
items.student_answer: SO2 is sulfur dioxide. It is a covalent compound made from sulfur and oxygen.

items.name: carbon monoxide
items.student_answer: CO is carbon monoxide. It is a covalent compound made from carbon and oxygen. CO is different from CO2.

items.name: potassium chloride
items.student_answer: KCl is potassium chloride. It is an ionic compound made from potassium ions and chloride ions.

items.name: iron(III) oxide
items.student_answer: Fe2O3 is iron(III) oxide. It is commonly associated with rust and contains iron and oxygen.
